functions.name: pyshex.evaluate.evaluate
documentation:
Evaluate focus node `focus` in graph `g` against shape `shape` in ShEx schema `schema`

```
param g

Graph containing RDF

param schema

ShEx Schema -- if str, it will be parsed

param focus

focus node in g. If not specified, all URI subjects in G will be evaluated.

param start

Starting shape.  If omitted, the Schema start shape is used

param debug_trace

Turn on debug tracing

return

None if success or failure reason if failure
```

modules.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_6_schema_requirements
documentation:
Implemention of `5.6 Schema Requirements <http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#schema-requirements>`_

The semantics defined above assume two structural requirements beyond those imposed by the grammar of the
abstract syntax. These ensure referential integrity and eliminate logical paradoxes such as those that arrise
through the use of negation. These are not constraints expressed by the schema but instead those imposed on
the schema.

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_6_schema_requirements.conforms
documentation:
`5.6.1 Schema Validation Requirement <http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#validation-requirement>`_

A graph G is said to conform with a schema S with a ShapeMap m when:

Every, SemAct in the startActs of S has a successful evaluation of semActsSatisfied.
Every node n in m conforms to its associated shapeExprRefs sen where for each shapeExprRef sei in sen:
    sei references a ShapeExpr in shapes, and
    satisfies(n, sei, G, m) for each shape sei in sen.

:return:

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_6_schema_requirements.valid_shape_references
documentation: `5.6.2 Shape Expression Reference Requirement <http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#shapeExprRef-requirement>`_

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_6_schema_requirements.valid_triple_references
documentation: `5.6.3 Triple Expression Reference Requirement <http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#tripleExprRef-requirement>`_

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_6_schema_requirements.valid_negations

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_2_validation_definition.isValid
documentation:
[5.2 Validation Definition](http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#validation)

The expression isValid(G, m) indicates that for every nodeSelector/shapeLabel pair (n, s) in m, s has a

: corresponding shape expression se and satisfies(n, se, G, m). satisfies is defined below for each form
  of shape expression

```
param cntxt

evaluation context - includes graph and schema

param m

list of NodeShape pairs to test

return

Success/failure indicator and, if fail, a list of failure reasons
```

modules.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_7_semantic_actions
documentation:
Implementation of `5.7 Semantic Actions <http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#semantic-actions>`_

A stub for the moment.

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_7_semantic_actions.semActsSatisfied
documentation:
`5.7.1 Semantic Actions Semantics <http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#semantic-actions-semantics>`_

The evaluation semActsSatisfied on a list of SemActs returns success or failure. The evaluation of an individual
SemAct is implementation-dependent.

modules.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p3_terminology
documentation:
Implementation of `3. Terminology <http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#terminology>`_

Shape expressions are defined using terms from RDF semantics [rdf11-mt]:

* Node: one of IRI, blank node, Literal
* Graph: a set of Triples of (subject, predicate, object)

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p3_terminology.arcsOut
documentation: arcsOut(G, n) is the set of triples in a graph G with subject n.

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p3_terminology.predicatesOut
documentation: predicatesOut(G, n) is the set of predicates in arcsOut(G, n).

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p3_terminology.arcsIn
documentation: arcsIn(G, n) is the set of triples in a graph G with object n.

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p3_terminology.predicatesIn
documentation: predicatesIn(G, n) is the set of predicates in arcsIn(G, n).

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p3_terminology.neigh
documentation:
neigh(G, n) is the neighbourhood of the node n in the graph G.

neigh(G, n) = arcsOut(G, n) ∪ arcsIn(G, n)

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p3_terminology.predicates
documentation:
redicates(G, n) is the set of predicates in neigh(G, n).

predicates(G, n) = predicatesOut(G, n) ∪ predicatesIn(G, n)

modules.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_3_shape_expressions
documentation: Implementation of `5.3 Shape Expressions <http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#node-constraint-semantics>`_

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_3_shape_expressions.satisfies
documentation:
[5.3 Shape Expressions](http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#node-constraint-semantics)

satisfies: The expression satisfies(n, se, G, m) indicates that a node n and graph G satisfy a shape

: > expression se with shapeMap m.

  satisfies(n, se, G, m) is true if and only if:

  > - Se is a NodeConstraint and satisfies2(n, se) as described below in Node Constraints.
  >   : Note that testing if a node satisfies a node constraint does not require a graph or shapeMap.
  > - Se is a Shape and satisfies(n, se) as defined below in Shapes and Triple Expressions.
  > - Se is a ShapeOr and there is some shape expression se2 in shapeExprs such that
  >
  > satisfies(n, se2, G, m).
  > \* Se is a ShapeAnd and for every shape expression se2 in shapeExprs, satisfies(n, se2, G, m).
  > \* Se is a ShapeNot and for the shape expression se2 at shapeExpr, notSatisfies(n, se2, G, m).
  > \* Se is a ShapeExternal and implementation-specific mechansims not defined in this specification
  > indicate success.
  > \* Se is a shapeExprRef and there exists in the schema a shape expression se2 with that id and
  >
  > > satisfies(n, se2, G, m).

:::{note}
Where is the documentation on recursion? All I can find is
[5.9.4 Recursion Example](http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#example-recursion)
:::

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_3_shape_expressions.satisifesShapeOr
documentation: Se is a ShapeOr and there is some shape expression se2 in shapeExprs such that satisfies(n, se2, G, m).

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_3_shape_expressions.satisfiesShapeAnd
documentation: Se is a ShapeAnd and for every shape expression se2 in shapeExprs, satisfies(n, se2, G, m)

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_3_shape_expressions.satisfiesShapeNot
documentation: Se is a ShapeNot and for the shape expression se2 at shapeExpr, notSatisfies(n, se2, G, m)

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_3_shape_expressions.satisfiesExternal
documentation:
Se is a ShapeExternal and implementation-specific mechansims not defined in this specification indicate
success.

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_3_shape_expressions.satisfiesShapeExprRef
documentation:
Se is a shapeExprRef and there exists in the schema a shape expression se2 with that id
and satisfies(n, se2, G, m).

modules.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_4_node_constraints
documentation: Implementation of `5.4 <http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#node-constraints>`_

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_4_node_constraints.satisfiesNodeConstraint
documentation:
`5.4.1 Semantics <http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#node-constraint-semantics>`_

For a node n and constraint nc, satisfies2(n, nc) if and only if for every nodeKind, datatype, xsFacet and
values constraint value v present in nc nodeSatisfies(n, v). The following sections define nodeSatisfies for
each of these types of constraints:

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_4_node_constraints.nodeSatisfiesNodeKind
documentation:
`5.4.2 Node Kind Constraints <http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#nodeKind>`_

For a node n and constraint value v, nodeSatisfies(n, v) if:

    * v = "iri" and n is an IRI.
    * v = "bnode" and n is a blank node.
    * v = "literal" and n is a Literal.
    * v = "nonliteral" and n is an IRI or blank node.

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_4_node_constraints.nodeSatisfiesDataType
documentation:
`5.4.3 Datatype Constraints <http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#datatype>`_

For a node n and constraint value v, nodeSatisfies(n, v) if n is an Literal with the datatype v and, if v is in
the set of SPARQL operand data types[sparql11-query], an XML schema string with a value of the lexical form of
n can be cast to the target type v per XPath Functions 3.1 section 19 Casting[xpath-functions]. Only datatypes
supported by SPARQL MUST be tested but ShEx extensions MAY add support for other datatypes.

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_4_node_constraints.nodeSatisfiesStringFacet
documentation:
`5.4.5 XML Schema String Facet Constraints <ttp://shex.io/shex-semantics/#xs-string>`_

String facet constraints apply to the lexical form of the RDF Literals and IRIs and blank node
identifiers (see note below regarding access to blank node identifiers).

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_4_node_constraints.nodeSatisfiesNumericFacet
documentation:
`5.4.5 XML Schema Numeric Facet Constraints <http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#xs-numeric>`_

Numeric facet constraints apply to the numeric value of RDF Literals with datatypes listed in SPARQL 1.1
Operand Data Types[sparql11-query]. Numeric constraints on non-numeric values fail. totaldigits and
fractiondigits constraints on values not derived from xsd:decimal fail.

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_4_node_constraints.nodeSatisfiesValues
documentation:
`5.4.5 Values Constraint <http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#values>`_

For a node n and constraint value v, nodeSatisfies(n, v) if n matches some valueSetValue vsv in v.

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_4_node_constraints._nodeSatisfiesValue
documentation:
A term matches a valueSetValue if:
: - vsv is an objectValue and n = vsv.
  - vsv is a Language with langTag lt and n is a language-tagged string with a language tag l and l = lt.
  - vsv is a IriStem, LiteralStem or LanguageStem with stem st and nodeIn(n, st).
  - vsv is a IriStemRange, LiteralStemRange or LanguageStemRange with stem st and exclusions excls and
    nodeIn(n, st) and there is no x in excls such that nodeIn(n, excl).
  - vsv is a Wildcard with exclusions excls and there is no x in excls such that nodeIn(n, excl).

Note that ObjectLiteral is *not* typed in ShExJ.jsg, so we identify it by a lack of a 'type' variable

:::{note}
Mismatch with spec
This won't work correctly if the stem value is passed in to nodeIn, as there will be no way to know whether
we're matching an IRI or other type
:::

... note:: Language issue

: The stem range spec shouldn't have the first element in the exclusions

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_4_node_constraints.nodeInIriStem
documentation:
**nodeIn**: asserts that an RDF node n is equal to an RDF term s or is in a set defined by a
{py:class}`ShExJ.IriStem`, {py:class}`LiteralStem` or {py:class}`LanguageStem`.

The expression `nodeInIriStem(n, s)` is satisfied iff:
: 1. `s` is a {py:class}`ShExJ.WildCard` or
  2. `n` is an {py:class}`rdflib.URIRef` and fn:starts-with(`n`, `s`)

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_4_node_constraints.nodeInLiteralStem
documentation:
<http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#values>

**nodeIn**: asserts that an RDF node n is equal to an RDF term s or is in a set defined by a
{py:class}`ShExJ.IriStem`, {py:class}`LiteralStem` or {py:class}`LanguageStem`.

The expression `nodeInLiteralStem(n, s)` is satisfied iff:
: 1. `s` is a {py:class}`ShExJ.WildCard` or
  2. `n` is an {py:class}`rdflib.Literal` and fn:starts-with(`n`, `s`)

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_4_node_constraints.nodeInLanguageStem
documentation:
<http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#values>

**nodeIn**: asserts that an RDF node n is equal to an RDF term s or is in a set defined by a
{py:class}`ShExJ.IriStem`, {py:class}`LiteralStem` or {py:class}`LanguageStem`.

The expression `nodeInLanguageStem(n, s)` is satisfied iff:
: 1. `s` is a {py:class}`ShExJ.WildCard` or
  2. `n` is a language-tagged string and fn:starts-with(`n.language`, `s`)

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_4_node_constraints.nodeInBnodeStem
documentation:
<http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#values>

**nodeIn**: asserts that an RDF node n is equal to an RDF term s or is in a set defined by a
{py:class}`ShExJ.IriStem`, {py:class}`LiteralStem` or {py:class}`LanguageStem`.

The expression `nodeInBnodeStem(n, s)` is satisfied iff:
: 1. `s` is a {py:class}`ShExJ.WildCard` or
  2. `n` is a language-tagged string and fn:starts-with(`n.language`, `s`)

methods.name: pyshex.shex_evaluator.ShExEvaluator.__init__
documentation:
Evaluator constructor. All of the parameters below can be set in the constructor or at runtime

```
param rdf

RDF string, file name, URL or Graph for evaluation.

param schema

ShEx Schema to evaluate. Can be ShExC, ShExJ or a pre-parsed schema

param focus

focus node(s).  If absent, all non-BNode subjects in the graph are evaluated

param start

start node(s). If absent, the START node in the schema is used

param rdf_format

format for RDF. Default: "Turtle"

param debug

emit semi-helpful debug information

param debug

debug graph fetch calls

param over_slurp

Controls whether SPARQL slurper does exact or over slurps

param output_sink

Function for accepting evaluation results and returns whether to keep evaluating
```

If we are already in the process of evaluating (n,s), as indicated self.evaluating, we return our current

```
result of evaluation

return
```


classes.name: pyshex.shapemap_structure_and_language.p3_shapemap_structure.QueryVariable
documentation: Definition: Query Variable
A query variable is a member of the set V where V is infinite and disjoint from RDF-T.

classes.name: pyshex.shapemap_structure_and_language.p3_shapemap_structure.SparqlTriplePattern
documentation: https://www.w3.org/TR/sparql11-query/#sparqlTriplePatterns
Definition: Triple Pattern
A triple pattern is member of the set:
(RDF-T ∪ V) x (I ∪ V) x (RDF-T ∪ V)

classes.name: pyshex.shapemap_structure_and_language.p3_shapemap_structure.SubjectFocusPattern
documentation: A focus selector identifies the slot (subject or object) to be validated. A wildcard indicates that the slot may hold
any value. A triple pattern has exactly one focus selector. A triple pattern maps to a SPARQL triple pattern with
the following restrictions:
* V (the set of variables) is either a fresh variable or a known token to identify the focus node.
* The focus node token appears in either the subject or the object position.
* The predicate position is filled by an IRI (I in the SPARQL definitions).

classes.name: pyshex.shapemap_structure_and_language.p3_shapemap_structure.START
documentation: Use the start node specified in the ShEx

classes.name: pyshex.shapemap_structure_and_language.p3_shapemap_structure.ShapeAssociation
documentation: In this document, these members can be addressed with a '.' operator. For instance, a shape association A
would have an A.nodeSelector member.
If the status member is absent, the status is assumed to be "conformant". The reason and appInfo members may
also be absent but have no default value.

classes.name: pyshex.shapemap_structure_and_language.p3_shapemap_structure.QueryShapeMap
documentation: A query shapeMap is a ShapeMap in which each shape association has only the members nodeSelector
and shapeLabel.

classes.name: pyshex.shapemap_structure_and_language.p3_shapemap_structure.FixedShapeMap
documentation: A fixed ShapeMap is a query ShapeMap in which each nodeSelector is an RDF node. The ShEx validation
process takes as input a fixed ShapeMap.

classes.name: pyshex.shapemap_structure_and_language.p3_shapemap_structure.ResultShapeMap
documentation: A result ShapeMap is a fixed ShapeMap with the addition of optional members status, reason and appInfo.

modules.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_5_shapes_and_triple_expressions
documentation: Implementation of `5.5 Shapes and Triple Expressions <http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#shapes-and-TEs>`_

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_5_shapes_and_triple_expressions.satisfiesShape
documentation:
[5.5.2 Semantics](http://shex.io/shex-semantics/#triple-expressions-semantics)

For a node `n`, shape `S`, graph `G`, and shapeMap `m`, `satisfies(n, S, G, m)` if and only if:

- `neigh(G, n)` can be partitioned into two sets matched and remainder such that
  `matches(matched, expression, m)`. If expression is absent, remainder = `neigh(G, n)`.

```
param n

focus node

param S

Shape to be satisfied

param cntxt

Evaluation context

param c

Debug context

return

true iff 
```

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_5_shapes_and_triple_expressions.valid_remainder
documentation:
Let **outs** be the arcsOut in remainder: `outs = remainder ∩ arcsOut(G, n)`.

Let **matchables** be the triples in outs whose predicate appears in a TripleConstraint in `expression`. If
`expression` is absent, matchables = Ø (the empty set).

- There is no triple in **matchables** which matches a TripleConstraint in expression
- There is no triple in **matchables** whose predicate does not appear in extra.
- closed is false or unmatchables is empty

```
param cntxt

evaluation context

param n

focus node

param matchables

non-matched triples

param S

Shape being evaluated

return

True if remainder is valid
```

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_5_shapes_and_triple_expressions.matches
documentation:
**matches**: asserts that a triple expression is matched by a set of triples that come from the neighbourhood of a
node in an RDF graph. The expression `matches(T, expr, m)` indicates that a set of triples `T` can satisfy these
rules:

extras is a hint that, if present, can be used to bypass cardinality permutations.  Beware, however, that this
may make the semantic actions more complex.

* expr has semActs and `matches(T, expr, m)` by the remaining rules in this list and the evaluation
  of semActs succeeds according to the section below on Semantic Actions.
* expr has a cardinality of min and/or max not equal to 1, where a max of -1 is treated as unbounded, and T
  can be partitioned into k subsets T1, T2,…Tk such that min ≤ k ≤ max and for each Tn,
  `matches(Tn, expr, m)` by the remaining rules in this list.
* expr is a OneOf and there is some shape expression se2 in shapeExprs such that a matches(T, se2, m).
* expr is an EachOf and there is some partition of T into T1, T2,… such that for every expression
  expr1, expr2,… in shapeExprs, matches(Tn, exprn, m).
* expr is a TripleConstraint and:
    * T is a set of one triple. Let t be the soul triple in T.
    * t's predicate equals expr's predicate. Let value be t's subject if inverse is true, else t's object.
    * if inverse is true, t is in arcsIn, else t is in `arcsOut`.
    * either
        * expr has no valueExpr
        * or `satisfies(value, valueExpr, G, m).

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_5_shapes_and_triple_expressions.matchesCardinality
documentation:
Evaluate cardinality expression

expr has a cardinality of min and/or max not equal to 1, where a max of -1 is treated as unbounded, and
T can be partitioned into k subsets T1, T2,…Tk such that min ≤ k ≤ max and for each Tn,
matches(Tn, expr, m) by the remaining rules in this list.

documentation:
expr is an EachOf and there is some partition of T into T1, T2,… such that for every expression
expr1, expr2,… in shapeExprs, matches(Tn, exprn, m).

functions.name: pyshex.shape_expressions_language.p5_5_shapes_and_triple_expressions.matchesTripleConstraint
documentation:
expr is a TripleConstraint and:

* t is a triple
* t's predicate equals expr's predicate.
  Let value be t's subject if inverse is true, else t's object.
* if inverse is true, t is in arcsIn, else t is in arcsOut.

documentation:
expr is an tripleExprRef and satisfies(value, tripleExprWithId(tripleExprRef), G, m).
The tripleExprWithId function is defined in Triple Expression Reference Requirement below.

functions.name: pyshex.utils.datatype_utils.can_cast_to
documentation:
5.4.3 Datatype Constraints

Determine whether "a value of the lexical form of n can be cast to the target type v per
XPath Functions 3.1 section 19 Casting[xpath-functions]."

functions.name: pyshex.utils.datatype_utils.total_digits
documentation:
5.4.5 XML Schema Numberic Facet Constraints

totaldigits and fractiondigits constraints on values not derived from xsd:decimal fail.

functions.name: pyshex.utils.datatype_utils.fraction_digits
documentation:
5.4.5 XML Schema Numeric Facet Constraints

for "fractiondigits" constraints, v is less than or equals the number of digits to the right of the decimal place
in the XML Schema canonical form[xmlschema-2] of the value of n, ignoring trailing zeros.

functions.name: pyshex.utils.partitions.partition_t
documentation:
Partition T into all possible partitions of T of size nparts
\:param T: Set of RDF triples to be partitioned
\:param nparts: number of partitions (e.g. 2 means return all possible 2 set partitions
\:return: Iterator that returns partitions

We don't actually partition the triples directly -- instead, we partition a set of integers that
reference elements in the (ordered) set and return those
